titleStmt.title: Letter from Jane Coffey to her son Diarmid Coffey, 16 October 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Jane Coffey

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was addressed to Diarmid Coffey (1888-1964) from his mother Jane Coffey (née l'Estrange) (1857-1921). The letter refers to a planned trip to London. The trip is a welcome replacement for a journey to Wales, however, Jane feels that England is an unpleasant place for an 'Irish Irelander' right now.Diarmid Coffey was born on 24 December 1888 in Dublin. He was a public servant and an author. He was called to the bar in 1912 and later joined the Irish Volunteers as secretary to Col. Cotter. He died in 1964.

msIdentifier.collection: Coffey & Chenevix Trench Papers, Collection List No 153, 46,301/2

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
you addressed
yr letter to
Harcourt Terrace
wh delayed it late
it came this
afternoon!
too late to
write Langridge, Bath 16.10.16 Dearest D. Phyllis & Basil have
written that they come
out for weekend so
I have given up being
& hope to go
to London with them
on Monday to the Unions
& home Wed or Thursday â
It is a terrible journey
from here to Wales & I
am not sorry to give it
up â tho' I never would
neglect to see heng but
England is unpleasant
for an Irish Irelander
just now â Mrs Finlay u has written me
a most kind invitation
& they would be a bit
sympathetic â so expect
me home Wed eve of
next week â Anne left
today she was very nice
I find her more sympathetic
than Alec just now
and really will be glad
to be at home again
You can tell Margaret to
have something in âbut
there is plenty of time â
& will have had enough held
by Wed. I was very much
disappointed at no letter
from you today. Yr loving
Mother. no letter How good to meet soldiers
not imperialist â
envelope postmarked Bath
11.45am
17 Oct 16 Diarmid Coffey Esqu, Mount Trenchard, Foynes, Co. Limerick, Ireland
